arcs.title: The Sword Test Is Not A Receipt
lore_beats: Arthur's kingship is proved through the sword test, but public acceptance still depends on witnesses, politics, and competing versions.
The sword motif should remain unstable enough for variant quests: stone, anvil, lake, and royal proof can each matter differently.
arcs.summary: Players investigate the sword test as both miracle and political event, establishing that legitimacy in Camelot is fought over after the wonder happens.
arcs.original_hook: The under-market sells forged witness marks, forcing players to decide whether Camelot needs clean truth, useful truth, or a dangerous public contradiction.

steps.title: Collect Witness Marks
steps.objective: Find three incompatible accounts of the sword test.

steps.title: Audit False Proof
steps.objective: Compare public testimony against Merlin's missing ledger and the under-market's forged marks.

steps.title: Choose The Public Version
steps.objective: Publish a version that shapes which factions accept Arthur first.

arcs.title: Merlin's Ledger Has Teeth
lore_beats: Merlin engineers, interprets, or warns around Arthur's rise depending on the source tradition.
Prophecy should be useful, costly, and incomplete rather than a simple spoiler.
arcs.summary: Players treat Merlin's prophecies as operating records: debts, bargains, hidden warnings, and omissions that can save Camelot or bankrupt it morally.
arcs.original_hook: Merlin's old accounting system has been copied by clerks who sell prophecies as contracts, and some contracts appear to sign back.

steps.title: Find The Empty Chair
steps.objective: Inspect Merlin's absent office and recover the first prophecy debt.

steps.title: Settle A Warning
steps.objective: Use or suppress a warning that would embarrass a future knight.

steps.title: Mark The Cost
steps.objective: Record who pays when prophecy prevents one disaster and invites another.

arcs.title: The Scabbard Problem
lore_beats: Excalibur's scabbard is treated as a protective treasure whose loss matters as much as the sword's fame.
Arthurian relic quests work best when power and protection are separate questions.
arcs.summary: Players discover that Camelot overvalues famous weapons while ignoring the lost protections that keep a kingdom alive.
arcs.original_hook: Relic dealers advertise blade fragments while a quieter ring trades maps to the missing scabbard, each map demanding a confession of loss.

steps.title: Inspect The Empty Mount
steps.objective: Learn why the missing scabbard frightens careful people more than a stolen blade.

steps.title: Recover The Map Scrap
steps.objective: Claim the map scrap without letting a relic dealer define its meaning.

steps.title: Open The Avalon Wall
steps.objective: Use the map scrap to prove Avalon is a route the world can negotiate with.

arcs.title: The Table Makes Rivals Sit Still
lore_beats: The Round Table gathers heroic peers, but kinship, honor, rivalry, and private vows keep producing instability.
The fellowship should function as both ideal and pressure cooker.
arcs.summary: Players manage the Round Table as a living faction network where honor checks, seat claims, and rumor economies change who will stand together.
arcs.original_hook: Every seat has a shadow claimant sponsored by lenders, prophets, or grieving families, so the table's equality has to be maintained like a dangerous engine.

steps.title: Answer A Seat Claim
steps.objective: Face a claimant whose legal proof is weak but whose grievance is real.

steps.title: Trace The Sponsor
steps.objective: Find who profits when knights confuse honor with unpaid debt.

steps.title: Keep The Table Breathing
steps.objective: Choose a compromise that preserves fellowship while leaving a future cost on the board.

arcs.title: The Grail Sorts The Room
lore_beats: The Grail quest tests spiritual fitness, wounded kingship, purity, perception, and the limits of ordinary chivalric ambition.
Galahad, Percival, and other Grail figures should create different quest lenses rather than one fixed route.
arcs.summary: Players encounter the Grail as a force that sorts witnesses, not as a prize that can simply be taken.
arcs.original_hook: A sacred choir begins selecting the wrong people by worldly standards, exposing which heroic reputations were built for applause rather than repair.

steps.title: Hear The Unseen Cup
steps.objective: Stand where the Grail is heard before it is seen and record who can bear the sound.

steps.title: Test The Witnesses
steps.objective: Let chance and character decide whether the witness is ready, ambitious, frightened, or useful.

steps.title: Send The Wound Outward
steps.objective: Let the Grail wound disturb cross-legend roads so other myth packs can react.

arcs.title: Mordred Files The Crown
lore_beats: Mordred's role varies by tradition, but succession pressure, betrayal, and final conflict are central Arthurian beats.
The fall of Camelot should grow from accumulated obligations, not a single surprise villain switch.
arcs.summary: Players track Mordred's claim as paperwork, rumor, bloodline, opportunism, and a crisis that feeds on every unresolved faction debt.
arcs.original_hook: Mordred's clerks do not need to lie if Camelot has already produced enough contradictory truths to weaponize.

steps.title: Read Rival Writs
steps.objective: Compare claims to the crown and identify which earlier player choices made them stronger.

steps.title: Audit Faction Debts
steps.objective: Discover which knights, clerks, and foreign envoys will change sides if debts come due.

steps.title: Choose A Counterclaim
steps.objective: Answer Mordred with law, mercy, exposure, or force, each carrying a different road to Camlann.

arcs.title: Avalon Refuses The Ending
lore_beats: Avalon holds the ambiguity of Arthur's departure, survival, healing, and possible return.
The ending should preserve mythic uncertainty while still resolving player choices.
arcs.summary: Players reach Avalon as a contested mercy where factions want proof of death, proof of return, or control over the story between them.
arcs.original_hook: Avalon behaves like a court that only hears unfinished cases, so the player's record of contradictions becomes the passport.

steps.title: Pay The Passage
steps.objective: Pay Avalon's price with a truth the player chose not to simplify earlier.

steps.title: Answer The Court
steps.objective: Present the record of Camelot's contradictions without pretending they are clean.

steps.title: Leave The Question
steps.objective: Resolve the campaign by choosing which uncertainty the world must live with.

arcs.title: The Under-Market Prices Miracles
lore_beats: Arthurian relics carry political and spiritual meaning beyond combat utility.
False relics and partial relics can create useful procedural uncertainty.
arcs.summary: Players investigate a relic trade that turns public faith into inventory, revealing which factions profit from confusion around Excalibur, the scabbard, and the Grail.
arcs.original_hook: Camelot's under-market offers honest prices for dishonest miracles, and some false relics still do real damage.

steps.title: Price A Fragment
steps.objective: Learn what different factions will pay for a supposed Excalibur fragment.

steps.title: Test The Receipt
steps.objective: Use chance to decide whether the receipt, the relic, or the buyer is cursed.

arcs.title: Welsh Court Echoes
lore_beats: Welsh and broader Celtic materials preserve different textures for Arthurian courts, marvels, kinship, and honor.
Arthur should not be flattened into only one later chivalric mode.
arcs.summary: Players follow older court echoes that complicate Camelot's polished version of itself.
arcs.original_hook: A visiting storyteller accepts payment only in corrected boasts, forcing Camelot's knights to admit which old names they borrowed from.

steps.title: Hear The Older Boast
steps.objective: Record a Welsh court echo that disagrees with Camelot's polished account.

steps.title: Pay In Corrected Boasts
steps.objective: Trade reputation for a truer account of marvel, kinship, or honor.

arcs.title: Morgan's Bargains
lore_beats: Morgan le Fay shifts across traditions as healer, antagonist, enchantress, political actor, and otherworldly presence.
Morgan arcs should allow ambiguous help and harm rather than one fixed alignment.
arcs.summary: Players encounter Morgan through bargains that may heal, expose, punish, or preserve Camelot depending on what the player owes.
arcs.original_hook: Morgan's agents do not ask whether a bargain is good or evil; they ask whether Camelot read the small print before needing rescue.

steps.title: Receive The Small Print
steps.objective: Accept or reject a bargain that can point toward the scabbard at a moral cost.

steps.title: Test Help Or Harm
steps.objective: Let the bargain's result depend on prior debts, chance, and faction reputation.

arcs.title: Gawain's Kinship Web
lore_beats: Gawain and his kin create dense webs of loyalty, vengeance, honor, and rivalry across Arthurian stories.
Family obligations can make a heroic choice costly even when it is correct.
arcs.summary: Players handle kinship claims around Gawain's circle, where every settlement strengthens one loyalty and insults another.
arcs.original_hook: A family debt ledger claims to be older than the Round Table and refuses to recognize the table's authority without compensation.

steps.title: Map The Family Debt
steps.objective: Build a kinship debt map before judging a dispute.

steps.title: Choose Honor Or Mercy
steps.objective: Resolve a dispute in a way that creates a future ally and a future grievance.

arcs.title: Grail Witnesses
lore_beats: Grail traditions emphasize witness, worthiness, wounds, and spiritual perception.
Multiple Grail figures should produce multiple kinds of playable testimony.
arcs.summary: Players gather witnesses whose accounts of the Grail disagree because each saw only what they were able to bear.
arcs.original_hook: The Grail choir sells no answers, but it will rent the player three silences, each from a different witness.

steps.title: Rent A Silence
steps.objective: Choose which witness silence to preserve and which to break.

steps.title: Compare Visions
steps.objective: Turn incompatible Grail visions into branching procedural quest states.

arcs.title: Cross-Myth Leaks
lore_beats: Arthurian roads to Avalon and otherworldly sites create a natural bridge to connected legend packs.
Cross-legend play should be earned through routes, witnesses, messengers, curses, and relic consequences.
arcs.summary: Players stabilize leaks between Arthurian routes and other myth regions so future packs can react through tags rather than hardcoded pairings.
arcs.original_hook: Avalon is not the only place with strange ferries, and the first foreign messenger arrives carrying a receipt for a curse Camelot has not issued yet.

steps.title: Identify The Leak
steps.objective: Determine whether the cross-myth route is a dream, ferry, curse, or invitation.

steps.title: Tag The World State
steps.objective: Emit shared tags that Greek, Celtic, Norse, South Asian, and later packs can respond to.
